methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Comand AI is a seed-stage defense and security frontier-tech company founded by Loïc Mougeolle and Félix Petersen, having raised €11.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security, supplemented by a broader comp set of eight private companies and two public equities (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, anchors to a median valuation of $150M across the five comparables. That peer set spans Tenna Systems at $60M (seed stage) through Stark Defence at $500M (Series A), with intermediate valuations at NODA AI ($125M), Ares Industries ($150M), and Vatn Systems ($300M). The $60M–$500M range reflects material dispersion within the peer cohort, consistent with stage and market positioning variance in early-stage defense tech.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M–$172M, centered on $150M. This range is narrowed by the single-method constraint; multiple triangulation approaches would reduce uncertainty. Key downside risks include execution risk at seed stage, regulatory pathway clarity for defense applications, and customer concentration. Upside hinges on proof-of-concept validation and defense-sector adoption velocity. The median estimate implies a 13x multiple on capital deployed to date, materially higher than typical seed multiples, warranting scrutiny of comparable risk profiles and growth trajectory assumptions.
